title: Robotic Cell Cycle Time Deviation
app: Splunk OT Intelligence, robot controller HEC
spl:
index=mfg sourcetype="robot:cycle"
| eval delta_sec=cycle_sec-target_sec, delta_pct=if(target_sec>0, 100*delta_sec/target_sec, null)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats avg(cycle_sec) as avg_cycle, avg(target_sec) as avg_target, stdev(cycle_sec) as cycle_jitter by cell_id, program_id, _time
| eval avg_delta_pct=if(avg_target>0, 100*(avg_cycle-avg_target)/avg_target, null)
| where avg_delta_pct>10 OR cycle_jitter>3
| table _time, cell_id, program_id, avg_cycle, avg_target, avg_delta_pct, cycle_jitter
description: Drift from takt-time signals tooling wear or program changes; catching deviation early avoids quality escapes and unplanned maintenance.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Robotic Cell Cycle Time Deviation to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Ingest cycle completion messages from robot OEM or PLC via Edge Hub syslog. Baseline `target_sec` per program revision in lookup when recipes change. Alert maintenance when sustained positive delta exceeds policy. **Domain context:** Robot takt-time drift often tracks EOAT wear, TCP calibration, or upstream material variation—correlate with CMMS on the same `cell_id`. **Splunk:** OEM syslog formats vary; use `props.conf` to extract `cycle_sec`/`program_id` consistently across firmware versions.
visualization: Line chart (cycle time vs target), Control chart, Table (cells in deviation), Bar chart (delta by program).
knownFalsePositives: Cycle noise during teach-in, light-curtain breaks, or dual-tool changeovers that the robot log shows as normal protective stops, not a fault.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Intelligence, robot controller HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=mfg` `sourcetype="robot:cycle"` (cell_id, program_id, cycle_sec, target_sec).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest cycle completion messages from robot OEM or PLC via Edge Hub syslog. Baseline `target_sec` per program revision in lookup when recipes change. Alert maintenance when sustained positive delta exceeds policy. **Domain context:** Robot takt-time drift often tracks EOAT wear, TCP calibration, or upstream material variation—correlate with CMMS on the same `cell_id`. **Splunk:** OEM syslog formats vary; use `props.conf` to extract `cycle_sec`/`program_id` consistently across firmware versions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mfg sourcetype="robot:cycle"
| eval delta_sec=cycle_sec-target_sec, delta_pct=if(target_sec>0, 100*delta_sec/target_sec, null)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats avg(cycle_sec) as avg_cycle, avg(target_sec) as avg_target, stdev(cycle_sec) as cycle_jitter by cell_id, program_id, _time
| eval avg_delta_pct=if(avg_target>0, 100*(avg_cycle-avg_target)/avg_target, null)
| where avg_delta_pct>10 OR cycle_jitter>3
| table _time, cell_id, program_id, avg_cycle, avg_target, avg_delta_pct, cycle_jitter
```

Understanding this SPL

**Robotic Cell Cycle Time Deviation** — Drift from takt-time signals tooling wear or program changes; catching deviation early avoids quality escapes and unplanned maintenance.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mfg` `sourcetype="robot:cycle"` (cell_id, program_id, cycle_sec, target_sec). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Intelligence, robot controller HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mfg; **sourcetype**: robot:cycle. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=mfg, sourcetype="robot:cycle". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cell_id, program_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **avg_delta_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where avg_delta_pct>10 OR cycle_jitter>3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Robotic Cell Cycle Time Deviation**): table _time, cell_id, program_id, avg_cycle, avg_target, avg_delta_pct, cycle_jitter


Step 3 — Validate
Compare calculated buckets with the MES shift report or historian (for example PI or Proficy) for the same line and period. Confirm planned versus run time matches the routing master used by production.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (cycle time vs target), Control chart, Table (cells in deviation), Bar chart (delta by program).
grandmaExplanation: We watch robot cycle times against normal. We help you catch wear or program drift before throughput drops.
